programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: Call of The Loon
programme.desc: Sitting on the shores of Moosehead Lake, the cabin known as the 'Call of The Loon' has been reacquired by the original builder's great-granddaughter. Chase and the team update the amenities and add a second story to accommodate the large family.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Builder Chase Morrill teams up with his brother, sister and best friend to save and transform abandoned cabins buried deep in the woods. They'll give these properties the facelift they've needed for decades. And you never know what you might find.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters: An Uphill Battle
programme.desc: Lifelong Mainers bought their Belgrade cabin in 1987 and have maintained it over the years, but the list of needed repairs continues to grow. Chase and the team give the camp much-needed life by rebuilding its front wall and replacing the heating system.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Rhapsody in Beige
programme.desc: Tamara transforms a contemporary home on a large wooded lot that's devoid of any character. She installs huge windows to bring the outdoors inside, but she faces issues with resurfacing a fireplace veneer.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: A Pain in the Deck
programme.desc: Continuing renovations on a large and bland contemporary home, Tamara tackles the master suite and replaces lots of dated cabinetry to make a stylish bathroom. She also creates a home heater and an immense deck and patio.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Bold In, Old Out
programme.desc: Tamara uses her design expertise to modernize an outdated, 1970s Tudor home.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Catwalk Craziness
programme.desc: Tamara Day starts on a 1900 Kansas City Shirtwaist-style home containing a living room with a catwalk instead of a ceiling. She pulls up linoleum flooring and gives the damaged foyer staircase a facelift.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: When One Barn Door Closes
programme.desc: After restoring a fourth bedroom in the Shirtwaist home, Tamara creates a reading nook and closet to complete the room. Then the Kansas City weather creates a problem in the front yard, while two vanities might not fit in the master bath.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Tamara's Tudor Tutorial
programme.desc: Tamara Day takes on a 1927 Tudor by blowing out the existing small kitchen and outfitting it with a large island and eating nook. She transforms a bedroom into a dining room, but her hopes of refurbishing the deck begin to break apart.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Shower Showstopper
programme.desc: Tamara takes on a behemoth 1927 Tudor house, but while she admires the home's original details, her dad discovers a problem. She uses a kitchenette to create a bar, revamps an uneven patio and enlarges the master bathroom.

programme.title: Salvage Dawgs: Splitting Up Jobs
programme.desc: Robert sends Tay and Grayson to salvage in Salem, Virginia, until the crew comes together to help finish the job. Mike and Ted travel to Norfolk, Virginia, to pick up a special nautical structure for a client.

programme.title: Paid Program
programme.desc: Paid programming.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: The Heart of the Home
programme.desc: Tamara Day tackles the battered kitchen of her new property, an 1897 bungalow. She enlarges and modernizes the space while preserving traditional elements of a bungalow kitchen.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: How Suite It Is
programme.desc: Tamara Day transforms a bedroom as she continues renovating an 1897 bungalow. Knowing modern buyers expect a master suite, Tamara annexes a second room to enlarge the tiny closet and add a bathroom.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: From Studs to Spindles
programme.desc: Tamara Day steps in to finish the renovation of a 1890s Craftsman-style home. This home was previously ripped down to the studs by a contractor before it was abandoned.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Book Lamps and Bubble Glass
programme.desc: Renovations on the 1890s Craftsman-style home continues as Tamara enlarges the master bedroom and creates a beautiful study. She runs into trouble when the antique tiles for the bathroom floor are not salvageable. Tamara finds a way to re-use old glass.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: The Trouble With Cobbles
programme.desc: Tamara continues work on a French chateau as she overhauls a large master suite covered in the tackiest wallpaper she's ever ripped out. She plans to move a bathroom to create an additional bedroom and extend a hallway.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: All Bricked Out
programme.desc: Tamara takes an 1892 brick Craftsman that was converted into three apartments and tries to restore it back into a single-family dwelling while keeping original details.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Fireplaces and Wired Spaces
programme.desc: Tamara annexes an oversized dining room to enlarge a tiny kitchen in a 1926 English Tudor. She rips out an outdated and tacky mantel to create a stunning fireplace surround, and then creates a large desktop for an in-home office.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Two-deck Tudor
programme.desc: Tamara struggles to create a more spacious master suite until she decides to add a second deck to a 1926 English Tudor. In the backyard, she adds an outdoor living area with a fire pit. She and her dad then realize the garage doors may cause a problem.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Move That Kitchen
programme.desc: Tamara renovates a single-story ranch and tackles her biggest challenge to date, transforming the former master bedroom into a stunning, open kitchen. However, she and her dad run into problems during demolition.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions: Copper Showstopper
programme.desc: Tamara continues renovations on the ranch by creating a new master suite. While polishing the copper fireplace hood, Tamara and her dad use a familiar solution that has unusual results. Her plans to create a sunroom run into problems due to weather.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Island Oasis
programme.desc: Beth wants a beach getaway on the white sandy beaches of Ambergris Caye, Belize. Ken Grissom and his crew transport building materials to the remote island and construct an off-the-grid luxury getaway with the Caribbean water just steps away.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Spearfish Canyon
programme.desc: Deep in the backwoods of Spearfish Canyon, South Dakota's premier off-grid builder Jared Capp sets out to turn four shipping containers into a stunning two-storey mountain hideaway.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Yurts So Good
programme.desc: Follow a young couple as they prep their own land and break ground to build their ultimate luxury yurt in the woods of Montana.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Rocky Mountain Homestead
programme.desc: With the help of a small crew, Cal and Billy Joe battle the elements as they race to finish Cal's cabin in time for hunting season. However, an unexpected setback threatens to halt the project entirely.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid: Montana Mountain Modern
programme.desc: A father-son duo builds an off-grid home in Montana's Flathead Valley. The 720-square-foot stick-frame home takes advantage of the mountain winds using a wind-turbine generator for power and features plenty of upcycled materials from the surrounding area.

programme.title: Man v. Food: Kansas City, Missouri
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels to Kansas City, Missouri in order to challenge three-pound almond butter and jelly sandwich known as the Elvis Challenge, a four-meat pizza with a tater top topper and a brisket sandwich piled high.

programme.title: Man v. Food: San Antonio, TX
programme.desc: Casey Webb's tasty travels take him to San Antonio, Texas. His first stop is San Antonio's oldest restaurant- a German deli- for the Wienerschnitzel.

programme.title: Good Eats: Wake Up Little Sushi
programme.desc: Alton investigates the ins and outs of ordering, eating, shopping for, and even making sushi.

programme.title: Good Eats: The Wing and I
programme.desc: Prepare America's most ubiquitous of bar foods, Buffalo chicken wings.

programme.title: Good Eats: Little Big Lunch: Eggs Benedict
programme.desc: Eggs Benedict is king of the brunch, so Alton rebuilds a 'Good Eats' version.

programme.title: Good Eats: My Shakshuka
programme.desc: Alton Brown takes a deep dive on one of the most internet-famous dishes of the decade by way of a famous film from the 1940s. Along the way, Alton talks through preserved lemons and homemade harissa.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Marseille
programme.desc: From beef stew to French pizza, Andrew Zimmern uncovers Marseille's edible icons.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Panama City
programme.desc: Andrew digs in to Panama City's classic tastes inspired by the blended cultures that make up the capital city's population. From fried whole fish and plantains to citric ceviche and yucca fritters, the Latin flair of this city seasons every dish.

programme.title: The Zimmern List: Providence
programme.desc: Andrew heads to Providence, RI, one of the oldest cities in America and home to a unique food scene. He savours authentic Portuguese-style seafood, discovers a refreshing take on New England pub food and is wowed by a one-of-a-kind tasting menu.

programme.title: The Zimmern List: St. Louis
programme.desc: Andrew heads to St. Louis to get his fill of comfort food. He savors barbecued pig snoots, indulges in a modern Midwestern incarnation of traditional Italian fare and slurps noodles at one of the hottest new spots for East Asian fare.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Original Inventions
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern looks back on favorite dishes that are also original inventions. Whether they were created out of necessity, the result of a happy accident or icons dreamed up for royalty, the origins of these edible icons are one-of-a-kind.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Guayaquil
programme.desc: Andrew introduces the tropical and frenetic coastal cuisine of Guayaquil, Ecuador. Bright flavours and local ingredients define their gastronomy, and Andrew tries everything from red mangrove crab to a plantain dumpling studded with crispy pork.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Portland, OR
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern shares the best of weird and wild Portland, OR, a city whose personality is apparent in its food culture. Local favorites include boozy doughnuts, Thai fish sauce chicken wings and even crawfish boils.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Shanghai
programme.desc: Andrew Zimmern introduces the popular tastes of Shanghai, China. From creamy hairy crab and savory pork-filled dumplings to rice wine-soaked chicken and scallion pancakes on the go, cuisine in this big city is sweet and simple.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Marseille
programme.desc: From beef stew to French pizza, Andrew Zimmern uncovers Marseille's edible icons.

programme.title: Bizarre Foods: Delicious Destinations: Panama City

programme.title: Unwrapped 2.0: Pop Stars
programme.desc: Alfonso Ribeiro explores some treats with serious pop. He'll pop the top of a Snapple Kiwi Strawberry beverage, pop a bubble with the classic Big League Chew shredded bubblegum, and get the inside scoop at Pez.

programme.title: Unwrapped 2.0: Little Bits
programme.desc: Alfonso Ribeiro explores little treats with big flavor like the colorful Carousel gumballs at Ford Gum, Ghirardelli Chocolate's decadent semi-sweet Baking Chips and the tasty Duchess Gem Donuts from Carolina Foods.

programme.title: Food: Fact or Fiction?: Gone Camping
programme.desc: Gear up and stake your tents as host Michael McKean sets up camp just in time for supper.

programme.title: Food: Fact or Fiction?: Brunch Beauties
programme.desc: Michael McKean skips breakfast and forgets about lunch so he can get to the bottom of brunch. He discovers how eggs Benedict was blessed by a pope and that we may have a melty mishap to thank for the croque monsieur.

programme.title: Food: Fact or Fiction?: Happy Birthday
programme.desc: Michael McKean makes everyone's birthday wishes come true as he celebrates the best holiday of the year. He discovers that birthday cake was born during a revolution and that a bowl of Chinese noodles can bring you more birthdays to celebrate.

programme.title: Food: Fact or Fiction?: Game Night
programme.desc: Michael McKean goes into the wild to hunt for juicy game meat myths and fresh fish fibs. He calls foul on duck a l'orange with its so-called French origins and throws a line at wicked tuna to find out how we got hooked on tuna melts.

programme.title: Food: Fact or Fiction?: Wicked Good
programme.desc: Michael McKean celebrates Halloween by investigating dishes that are so good they're downright wicked! He's uncovering rhubarb pie's paranormal proclivities and does a background check on ramen with its racy rap sheet.

programme.title: Food: Fact or Fiction?: Baked Goodies
programme.desc: Michael McKean throws the carb-free diet out the window and uncovers the stories behind our favorite baked goodies. He starts by revealing that cupcakes are edible equations and explaining how the whoopie pie nearly caused a civil war.

programme.title: Good Eats: Your Pad or Mine (Thai)
programme.desc: When his trip to Thailand is cancelled at the last minute, Alton sets out to find the next best thing a proper Pad Thai.

programme.title: Good Eats: The Waffle Truth
programme.desc: Alton teaches us how to build a better batter for waffles.

programme.title: Good Eats: Shell Game IV (Scallops)
programme.desc: Tales of a monster scallop send Alton traveling to a small town in Maine to investigate. After meeting with the nervous locals, he sets sail with Capt. Squint upon his house boat, the Okra where he's forced to cook a whole lot of scallop dishes.

programme.title: Good Eats: If It Ain't Broccoli Don't Fix It
programme.desc: Alton Brown transforms broccoli from limp and bitter to crisp and delicious.

programme.title: Good Eats: Crustacean Nation IV: Crawfish
programme.desc: Alton Brown discovers the sustainable alternative to lobster, the crawfish.

programme.title: Good Eats: Feeling Punchy
programme.desc: Alton brings back punch into the spotlight.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Chicken
programme.desc: Roger Mooking travels across America in search of the 12 very best chicken dishes. From frying and baking to grilling, Roger is hanging out with chefs as they explore all the best ways to cook wings, pot pies, pizzas and other savory chicken dishes.

programme.title: Man's Greatest Food: Cheesy Dishes
programme.desc: Roger Mooking says 'Cheese' as he explores 15 of the best cheesy dishes across the U.S. The creamiest, smoothest and stringiest French, Italian, Mexican and American cheesy classics and innovations are on display.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Wine Country Cookouts
programme.desc: California's wine country is the perfect place for an outdoor cookout. The weather is gorgeous, the wine is simply divine, and the food is down-right delicious. Roger has been invited to two parties: a pig roast in Paso Robles and a surf and turf grill.

programme.title: Man Fire Food: Backyard Blowouts
programme.desc: Roger's in Southern California for two unbelievable backyard blowouts. Roger meets Chef Ben Ford in Tarzana.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Fast Feud/ Never Friending Story
programme.desc: Ronnie Anne and Sid try to take down the obnoxious burger restaurant that opened up across the street. After picking Par as his new best friend, Bobby realizes he may be in over his head.

programme.title: The Loud House: Linc or Swim/ Changing the Baby
programme.desc: Lincoln purchases a kiddie pool, and things get out of hand when all 10 of his sisters want to use it. After noticing that none of his sisters share his interests, Lincoln attempts to mold Lily into his mini-me.

programme.title: The Loud House: Kings of the Con
programme.desc: The girls get more attention when accompany Lincoln and Clyde to the Ace Savvy convention dressed as the Full deck.

programme.title: The Loud House: Out of the Picture/ Room With a Feud
programme.desc: Afraid they'll be forgotten, Lincoln and Clyde want to make a splash in the yearbook and crash group photos. Seeing his sisters fight with their roommates, Lincoln suggests a room shuffle based on a compatibility test.

programme.title: The Loud House: Potty Mouth/ 'L' Is for Love
programme.desc: When the kids hear Lily curse, they're worried they have been a bad influence on her. A love letter addressed to 'L' Loud shows up at the house and the family has to figure out who it was meant for.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Whale Watching/ Krusty Kleaners
programme.desc: Mr. Krabs hires Squidward to babysit Pearl for the night, but she just wants to sneak out to a cool teen party. SpongeBob spills a milkshake during a delivery.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Call the Cops/ Surf N' Turf
programme.desc: Plankton goes to jail, but the secret formula is taken as evidence so SpongeBob and Mr. Krabs plot to get it back. Sandy struggles to build a ship-in-a-bottle before a big contest, so SpongeBob calls in a little extra help.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Dear Vikings/ Ditchin'
programme.desc: When Krusty Krab starts a new promotion in honour of the Vikings, SpongeBob sets out to learn all he can about this civilization.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Trenchbillies/ Sponge-Cano
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick fall into a cavern where frightful Trenchbillies keep them hostage.

programme.title: It's Pony: Pet Pony/ Dog Day
programme.desc: When pets are banned from the building, Annie and Pony have to convince the landlord to reconsider. When the dog from next door, Pony's sworn enemy, comes to stay with them, Annie needs to find a way to make them get along.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Porous Pockets/ Choir Boys
programme.desc: Patrick finds himself alone when SpongeBob becomes the richest citizen in Bikini Bottom.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Yours, Mine and Mine/ Kracked Krabs
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick buy a toy and have to learn to share it.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Chaperone
programme.desc: When Mr. Krab's daughter Pearl is stood up for her prom, SpongeBob agrees to step in.

programme.title: Breadwinners: Night of the Living Bread
programme.desc: SwaySway and Buhdeuce invent a loaf of bread that is so irresistible it turns their customers into bread craving zombies.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Boating Buddies/ Krabby Kronicle
programme.desc: Squidward is forced to attend Boating School with SpongeBob.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: The Clash of Triton
programme.desc: SpongeBob wants his friends and family to help celebrate King Neptune's birthday.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Barnacle Face/ Pet Sitter Pat
programme.desc: The night before the big school dance, Mr. Krabs asks SpongeBob to help Pearl get rid of a barnacle on her face.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Battle of the Grandpas/ Prankaversary
programme.desc: A multi-generational Mexican-American family run a market in a big city.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Fast Feud/ Never Friending Story
programme.desc: Ronnie Anne and Sid try to take down the obnoxious burger restaurant that opened up across the street. After picking Par as his new best friend, Bobby realizes he may be in over his head.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: What's Love Gato Do With It/ Dial M. for Mustard
programme.desc: After a magician hypnotizes Bobby into thinking he's a cat, the family needs to snap him out of it. Ronnie Anne and friends try to uncover what happened to Bruno's hot dog cart.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Away Game/ Monster Cash
programme.desc: Abuela worries that Ronnie Anne and Bobby will want to live at their dad's new apartment permanently. Carl hosts a haunted tour based on Abuela's tale of 'El Cucuy' and learns that this story may not be fiction.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Croaked!/ New Haunts
programme.desc: Ronnie Anne and Sid teach Adelaide about Day of the Dead to help her get over the death of her frog. Then, when Ronnie Anne forgoes a family Halloween tradition for a 'cool' party, she learns 'cool' doesn't always mean 'fun'.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: V.I.Peeved/ Señor Class
programme.desc: When her blog is discovered by a famous musician, Carlota fears her family will embarrass her. Then, when Hector decides to finish his schooling, he become Ronnie Anne and Carl's new classmate.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Cursed!
programme.desc: Celebrity psychic Ernesto Estrella deems Great Lakes City to be bad luck and Abuela believes him.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Trend Game/ This Bird Has Flown
programme.desc: When Ronnie Anne realizes that she's not up to date on all of the latest trends, she seeks help from Carlota. Bobby struggles to find Sergio after wishing he would fly away and never come back.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: Patnocchio/ ChefBob
programme.desc: Plankton tells Patrick he's his conscience so he can convince him to steal him a Krabby Patty. SpongeBob gets stage fright and has to invent a new persona.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants: FarmerBob/ Gary and Spot
programme.desc: SpongeBob and Patrick work on Old Man Jenkins' farm. And, Sandy narrates the adventures of Gary and Spot.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Jeanette's Secret Garden/ Treasure Hunt
programme.desc: Jeanette decides to plant a garden on the school grounds, but Bocarter manages to install vending machines instead.When Alvin finds a treasure map in Miss Croner's basement, the Chipmunks set off in search of lost treasure.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: What a Gem/ Family Spirit
programme.desc: When Alvin sees a sparkling diamond buried in his neighbor's rose garden, he's sure she's responsible for the recent diamond heist. Alvin tries to get out of the annual family day picnic with Dave to go to the big soccer game.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Members Only/ The New Kid
programme.desc: Alvin helps the mathletes stand up to the school bullies, but in the process they become bullies themselves. Alvin befriends a lonely new kid in school, but Theodore discovers the new student may not be who he says he is.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Let Them Eat Crumbs/ Who Ghosts There
programme.desc: Eleanor has prepared a cake for a Bake-Off. But Brittany and Alvin come home and eat the cake without knowing its importance! Later, worried that Dave will move the family to his dream house, the boys make the house seem haunted.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Wacky Wednesday/ The Chipmunk and the Catfish
programme.desc: When one of Simon's inventions malfunctions, Jeanette and Alvin switch places. Theodore creates a fake alias online and pretends to be a girl.

programme.title: ALVINNN!!! and the Chipmunks: Going Green/ Tattle Tail
programme.desc: Jeanette signs the Chipmunks and Chipettes up for a green living competition. Alvin likes a girl at school so he agrees to look after her talking bird, but he's nervous about the bird blabbing when he says something damaging.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Fairly OddPet
programme.desc: Jealous that everyone else in Dimmsdale has a dog, Timmy adopts a fairy dog. But this proves to be a bigger challenge than Timmy expected.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Dinklescouts/ I Dream of Cosmo
programme.desc: Timmy wishes Mr. Dinkleberg would replace Dad as Squirrely Scout leader. Dad tries to regain Timmy's respect and his leader position.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Turner and Pooch/ Dumbbell Curve
programme.desc: Crocker seeks out a new source of magic from Timmy's house. Realizing it's Sparky, Crocker tries to turn him against Timmy. Crocker grades on a bell curve so Timmy wishes everyone was dumber than him.

programme.title: Fairly OddParents: Love at First Bark / Desperate Without Housewives
programme.desc: Dad forbids Sparky from dating Dinkleberg's new dog. Timmy wishes Dad would like the dog as much as Sparky. When Mom won't stop nagging Timmy and Dad, Timmy wishes all women away for one day.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Walk Don't Run/ Croaked
programme.desc: In order to buy a new skateboard, Ronnie Anne and Sid start a dog-walking business that quickly gets out of hand. Then, Ronnie, Anne and Sid teach Adelaide about Day of the Dead to help her get over the death of her frog.

programme.title: The Casagrandes: Flea Market/ Copy Can't
programme.desc: Bobby struggles to give up control of the mercado; Ronnie Anne notices that she's the only person Carlitos won't copy.

programme.title: The Loud House: Silence of the Luans/ Undercover Mom
programme.desc: With Luan locked in a cell in the basement, who is responsible for the April Fool’s Day pranks this year?/ Mom goes undercover as a student at the high school and becomes BFFs with Leni, Luna and Luan.

programme.title: The Barbarian and the Troll: I Will Survive
programme.desc: The questers finally reach Alvin’s lair, but must first overcome a series of traps before Brendar can finally reunite with her brother.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Boyfriend From 27,000 B.C./ Voyage to the Bottom of Buford
programme.desc: Boys go on an archaeological dig and find a real caveman; he falls in love with Candace.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Put That Putter Away/ Does This Duckbill Make Me Look Fat?
programme.desc: Candace is sick and recruits Stacy to bust Phineas and Ferb building mini-golf course.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Traffic Cam Caper/ Bowl-R-Rama Drama
programme.desc: Candace tried to prove her brothers inventions by getting footage from a traffic camera.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The Monster of Phineas-n-Ferbenstein/ Oil on Candace
programme.desc: Boys set off to Central America to help a Damsel in Distress Isabella.

programme.title: 101 Dalmatian Street: Diamond Dogs / Ride Along
programme.desc: Follow the shenanigans of Dolly and Dylan and their 97 siblings in Camden Town, London.

programme.title: Amphibia: New Wartwood/ Friend or Frobo
programme.desc: Intent on winning over Wartwood, Marcy makes a plan to improve the town. Then, Desperate for fun, Polly breaks out and finds an unlikely new friend in the process.

programme.title: Amphibia: Civil Wart/ Hop-Popular
programme.desc: Anne exposes Wartwood to a teen romance film, and the town is split on the movie's love triangle. After losing his stand, Hop Pop decides to run for mayor of Wartwood.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The Klimpaloon Ultimatum
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb must help Love Handel prove the existence of Klimpaloon, or else Love Handel will be barred from the world of fact-based song writing for all time.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The Chronicles of Meap
programme.desc: Space pod crashes carrying an unbearable cute alien, Meap; another adventure begins.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Operation Crumb Cake/ Mandace
programme.desc: Isabella sends a letter to Phineas admitting her true feelings, but instantly regrets her decision/ Doof invents an '-inator' to disguise himself as the local pizza delivery boy.

programme.title: DuckTales: McMystery at McDuck McManor
programme.desc: When Scrooge unexpectedly disappears during his birthday party, Huey, Dewey and Louie find themselves hosting a mystery party where everyone is a suspect.

programme.title: DuckTales: The Spear of Selene!
programme.desc: Donald is dragged into a feud between Scrooge and Zeus, while Dewey and Webby search for an artifact that may provide the truth about Dewey's mother.

programme.title: DuckTales: The Missing Links of Moorshire!
programme.desc: Dewey's natural golf abilities challenge Scrooge's ego while a seemingly innocent, mystical golf course leads the group into trouble.

programme.title: Parker Plays
programme.desc: YouTube star Parker Coppins shares his hilarious gameplay commentary.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: Whoa-Oh, I'm On A Roll
programme.desc: A compilation of top gaming videos from YouTube stars.

programme.title: Polaris: Player Select: Halloween Spooktacular
programme.desc: A compilation of top gaming videos from YouTube stars.

programme.title: Amphibia: Handy Anne/ Fort in the Road
programme.desc: Worried that something bad could happen while they are in Newtopia, Anne decides to disaster-proof the farm. Later, Sprig longs for exotic road trip adventures, but Hop Pop's strict road rules threaten to spoil all of his fun plans.

programme.title: Amphibia: Anne or Beast?/ Best Fronds
programme.desc: Sprig tries to capture a mysterious beast to prove that he's responsible; Sprig takes Anne swimming in a nearby lake to help her feel less homesick.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Carpet Diem
programme.desc: Mabel finds a way to swap minds with Dipper and the townsfolk, so she uses her new found ability to make a difference in people's lives.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Land Before Swine
programme.desc: When a prehistoric beast snatches Mabel’s beloved pig Waddles, the Pines family must journey to a land trapped in time to rescue him.

programme.title: The Owl House: A Lying Witch and a Warden
programme.desc: When 14-year-old outcast Luz discovers a portal to a new world, she meets a witch, Eda, and a demon named King. In order to get home, Luz has to help them with a mission.

programme.title: The Owl House: Witches Before Wizards
programme.desc: Luz receives a map for a mystical quest from an ancient wizard.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Split Personality/ Brain Drain
programme.desc: Candace accidentally gets hit by a 'splitter' ray which causes her to have two different personalities: Romantic Candace and Busting Candace. Then, Phineas and Ferb are sick with a cold, so they create a live television feed to their friends' homes.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Thanks But No Thanks/ Troy Story
programme.desc: Inspired by Candace’s book club selection, the boys decide to reenact the Trojan War from “The Illiad,” but with a Phineas and Ferb twist.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: A Tale of Two Stans
programme.desc: Stan decides to run for Mayor of Gravity Falls to earn the kids’ and the townsfolk respect.

programme.title: Gravity Falls: Roadside Attraction
programme.desc: Grunkle Stan takes the kids with him on a road-trip to sabotage all the other tourist traps in Oregon.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Swimming Fool/ Tilly's Goat
programme.desc: When the Greens visit an indoor pool, Cricket is challenged to jump off the high-dive. Tilly claims that their goat has the spirit of a dog and enters it in a dog show.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Shark Objects / Dream Weaver
programme.desc: When Greens try to spend an eventful day at the beach, Cricket tries to prank the beach-goers; Cricket discovers he can influence the family's dreams to disastrous real-life results.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Fast Foodie/ Spaghetti Theory
programme.desc: When a Burger Clown franchise opens next door, Cricket tries to prove to Bill that he can eat fast food for every meal with no ill effects. Then, Tilly and Cricket debate whether the lives of people in Big City are intertwined.

programme.title: Big City Greens: Harvest Dinner/ Winner Winner
programme.desc: Bill and Nancy prep a special harvest dinner, while Cricket and Gramma race Tilly to get a missing ingredient. At the community center, Tilly trains to become a winner.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Unfair Science Fair / Unfair Science Fair Redux
programme.desc: Two stepbrothers build various objects with materials found around their home.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Out to Launch
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb build a rocket ship to get a better look and their mom and dad's star.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Phineas and Ferb Star Wars
programme.desc: The story is set a couple summers ago in a galaxy far, far away when Phineas, Ferb and the gang are in a parallel universe during Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope. Phineas and Ferb are living just one moisture farm over from Luke.

programme.title: Baymax Dreams: Baymax Dreams of Bed Bugs
Baymax Dreams: Baymax Dreams of Too Many Baymaxes
Baymax Dreams: Baymax Dreams of Glitch
Phineas and Ferb: Interview With a Platypus/ Tip of the Day
programme.desc: Animal translator built so the boys can figure out what Perry is really trying to say.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Attack of the 50 Foot Sister/ Backyard Aquarium
programme.desc: The boys create a PhineasandFerbalizer to speed up the growing process of vegetables.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Day of the Living Gelatin!/ Elementary, My Dear Stacy
programme.desc: Pool filled with bouncy gelatin and it comes to life; later family takes trip to England.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Don't Even Blink/ Chez Platypus
programme.desc: A determined Candace decides to find out where her brothers creations disappear to, and Phineas and Ferb open a platypus themed restaurant.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Perry Lays an Egg/ Gaming the System
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb find an egg, which they believe belongs to Perry, so they build a mechanical contraption to care for it.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The Chronicles of Meap
programme.desc: Space pod crashes carrying an unbearable cute alien, Meap; another adventure begins.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Thaddeus and Thor/ De Plane! De Plane!
programme.desc: Candace, Phineas and Ferb compete with their look-alikes; Candace goes to a party.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Let's Take a Quiz/ At the Car Wash
programme.desc: Candace wants to win popularity by going on Phineas and Ferb's TV game show. Later, an automatic carwash is created to help Isabella and the Fireside girls meet their fundraising goals.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Oh, There You Are, Perry/ Swiss Family Phineas
programme.desc: A musical device is built to find Perry/ A trip at sea strands the family on an island.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The O.W.C.A. Files
programme.desc: When an evil mastermind from Major Monogram's past, Professor Parenthesis, launches an attack on The O.W.C.A. by incapacitating the animal agents with robotic fleas, only Agent P and his agent trainees can save the day.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: Hide and Seek/ That Sinking Feeling
programme.desc: Phineas and Ferb shrink their friends while playing hide and seek.

programme.title: Phineas and Ferb: The Baljeatles/ Vanessassary Roughness
programme.desc: Baljeet signs up for rock camp and Vanessa tries to convince her dad she is responsible.

programme.desc: Alan goes on a date with one of Charlie's gorgeous ex-girlfriends.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men: I Always Wanted a Shaved Monkey
programme.desc: Evelyn calls Charlie to harass him over not calling her, and Judith bills Alan for an expensive pair of tennis shoes she bought Jake. Charlie and Alan begin to take their frustration out on one another.

programme.title: Black-ish: Juneteenth

programme.desc: When Molly gets a cold before her first date with Mike, she takes cold medication to help her feel better with disastrous results.

programme.desc: The Legends make a last-ditch effort to keep Sara from being abducted. Sara grapples with what she has just discovered about herself, but is also shocked when Rory finds her on the mystery planet. Sara, Mick and Gary devise a plan to help defeat Bishop.

programme.desc: Alan prepares Jake for Judith's wedding, while Charlie continues his fling with Herb's sister Myra.
